prompt:
billing needs proportional allocation on the money package and I'd rather we get it right once than keep hand-rolling it in three services.

Story: an invoice total gets split across cost centers by ratio — e.g. 1.00 USD split [3,1,3]. Floats are banned in this package for good reason, so the requirement is exact: the parts must sum back to the original amount, cent for cent, every time. When the division doesn't come out even, distribute the leftover cents by the largest-remainder method (the slots that lost the biggest fraction get the spare cents), and break remainder ties toward the earlier slot so the result is stable — finance re-runs reports and diffs them.

So: Allocate(ratios []int) ([]Money, error) on Money, plus a Split(n int) convenience that divides into n equal shares (same leftover rule, spare cents land on the front). Zero ratios are legal in a mix and get exactly nothing; empty ratio list, any negative ratio, or all-zero ratios are an error. Negative amounts (refunds) allocate as the mirror image of the positive case. Currency carries through to every part.

allocate_test.go is the acceptance contract — it includes a sweep asserting no cent is ever created or destroyed. money_test.go covers the arithmetic we already ship; all of it keeps passing, and neither test file gets edited. Gate: go test -race -timeout 30s ./...
